content.problemStatement:
Reverse and print the string **"I love Python"**


Output Format
nohtyP evol I
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases.
content.outputFormat: Print the reversed string "nohtyP evol I" on its own line for each test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1, so the reversed constant string is printed once.

sampleTestCases.output:
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
sampleTestCases.explanation: T=3, the same reversed string is printed on three separate lines.

sampleTestCases.output:
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
sampleTestCases.explanation: T=5, the reversed string appears five times.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I

hidden.label: edge case
hidden.expectedOutput:
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput:
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I
nohtyP evol I

solution.approach: The output is a fixed constant string reversed from "I love Python". Store this constant ("nohtyP evol I") and simply print it T times, each on a new line. No additional computation is required.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time print per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    T = int(data[0])
    rev = "nohtyP evol I"
    out = "\n".join([rev] * T)
    sys.stdout.write(out)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
